specialism.name: repo-summary-generation
specialism.task:
Produce and maintain a dense, machine-readable index of this Julia repository under ai/repo-summary-<YYYY-MM-DD>/ so that other specialisms and coding agents can locate types, methods, testsets, example drivers, docs pages, figures, and semantic groupings without re-scanning the codebase. The snapshot directory is date-stamped; a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.json detects staleness and prompts the user to refresh.
This specialism produces four compact artifacts: a global index (modules, exports, environments), a semantic index (code grouped by purpose), a jump table (symbol-to-file:line plus testset and multi-dispatch clusters), and per-file summary pages. It is NOT a coding agent — it never edits source, tests, or docs. It edits only the ai/repo-summary-<date>/ directory (and, on first install, ai/README.md and .claude/settings.json).
The specialism operates in two modes from the same workflow: first-run mode (no artifacts exist or the most recent snapshot is older than 30 days) and incremental mode (a coding task just modified a known set of files; update only affected entries and leave the rest byte-identical).

workflow.step:
Compute today's date and determine mode.
today=$(date +%Y-%m-%d) target=ai/repo-summary-$today
If $target/INDEX.md already exists → incremental mode: identify changed files from the commit SHA recorded in INDEX.md frontmatter via: git diff --name-only <sha>..HEAD
If $target does not exist → check for any ai/repo-summary-*/ directory: ls -d ai/repo-summary-* 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -n1
If a recent snapshot (<= 30 days old, judged by its date suffix) is found → copy it into $target and switch to incremental mode, using the full diff from its commit SHA. Otherwise → first-run mode: create $target/ and build all four artifacts from scratch.

Discover modules.
grep -nE '^module ' src/
Record each module name with file:line in INDEX.md under "## Modules". Only files that contain a bare `module X` declaration are true modules; files that are bare includes (no module declaration) are noted as "included into <ModuleName>" in their per-file summary. Exclude editor backup files (*~) and scratch files (tmp*.jl).

Discover include structure from the package entry point.
grep -nE '^(include|# )' src/EnsembleKalmanProcesses.jl
(or whatever the top-level .jl matching the package name is — find it via grep -rl '^module EnsembleKalmanProcesses' src/)
The comment lines immediately preceding groups of include() calls name the semantic groupings. Use these comment headers as section headings in SEMANTIC.md. Record every included file and the group it belongs to.

Discover types in source.
grep -nrE '^(mutable )?struct |^abstract type ' src/
For each match, record the bare type name (strip {T,...} parameters), file, and line in JUMP.md under "## Types". Also note whether the type is concrete (struct / mutable struct) or abstract (abstract type).

Discover exported symbols.
grep -nrE '^export ' src/
Expand each export line into individual symbol names. List them per-module in INDEX.md under "## Exported symbols" and add each to JUMP.md as a "symbol" row if not already present from step 4.

Discover Base method extensions.
grep -nrE '^function Base\.' src/ grep -nrE '^Base\.' src/
Group results by Base function name (e.g., Base.show, Base.summary, Base.length, Base.getindex). Record in SEMANTIC.md under "## Base method extensions", one sub-list per Base function name, with file:line for each method. This captures multiple-dispatch clusters across files at the Base boundary.

Cluster non-Base multi-dispatch methods.
From JUMP.md candidate functions (exported names + grep for `^function ` in src/): grep -nrE '^function [a-z_A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_!]*' src/
For any function name appearing in two or more distinct files, record a cluster row in JUMP.md under "## Multi-dispatch clusters": | function | file | line | Group rows by function name so all method definitions for the same function are adjacent.

Discover testsets.
find test/ -name 'runtests.jl' | sort
For each runtests.jl: grep -nE '@testset' <file>
Record testset name + file:line in JUMP.md under "## Testsets" and in SEMANTIC.md under "## Testing". The top-level test/runtests.jl gets its own per-file summary noting which subdirectory runtests.jl files it dispatches to (via include() or @testset include()).

Discover example driver scripts.
find examples/ -name '*.jl' -not -path '*/output/*' | sort
For each .jl file in examples/, write a per-file summary (see step 13) noting: - which named experiment/subdirectory it belongs to - whether it is a main driver or a helper (heuristic: files named run_*.jl, main.jl, calibrate.jl, or whose first function definition is `main()` are drivers) - which Project.toml environment it depends on (nearest Project.toml up the directory tree)
Link every driver to its experiment group in SEMANTIC.md under "## Examples / drivers".

Discover all Project.toml environments.
find . -name 'Project.toml' -not -path './.git/*' | sort
For each, assign a one-line role tag: - repo root → "root package" - docs/ → "documentation build" - test/ → "test environment" - examples/<name>/ → "example: <name>" - .dev/ → "dev tooling"
Record as a table in INDEX.md under "## Environments": | path | role |

Discover docs pages.
find docs/src -name '*.md' | sort
For each .md file, record in its per-file summary: - top-level heading (first `# ` line) - kind: "topic page" (top-level docs/src/*.md), "API stub" (docs/src/API/*.md), "hand-written tutorial" (docs/src/examples/*.md), "Literate-generated" (docs/src/literated/*.md) - count of `@ref` cross-references: grep -c '@ref' <file>
In SEMANTIC.md under "## Documentation", group by subdirectory.

Verify figure placement.
find docs/src -type f \( -name '*.png' -o -name '*.svg' -o -name '*.pdf' \ -o -name '*.jpg' -o -name '*.jpeg' -o -name '*.gif' \)
For each figure file: - If it resides outside docs/src/assets/ → add an entry to INDEX.md under "## Figure-placement issues" as: INCOMPLETE: figure outside docs/src/assets/: <relative-path> - If it resides inside docs/src/assets/ → record it in INDEX.md under "## Figures in docs/src/assets/" with a "referenced by" annotation: grep -rln <basename> docs/src/ List every .md file that references it, or "(none)" if unlinked.

Generate (or regenerate) per-file summaries at:
ai/repo-summary-<date>/files/<flattened-path>.md
where <flattened-path> is the relative path from the repo root with each `/` replaced by `__`. Example: src/ParameterDistributions.jl → files/src__ParameterDistributions.md
Scope: every file found in steps 2–12 — i.e.: src/*.jl (source files, excluding *~ and tmp*.jl) test/**/runtests.jl (test drivers at every level) examples/**/*.jl (excluding examples/**/output/**) docs/src/**/*.md (all docs pages) every Project.toml found in step 10
Per-file summary format (see <formatting> for the exact template).
In incremental mode, regenerate ONLY files that appear in the diff from step 1. All other summary files must be left byte-identical.

Write or overwrite INDEX.md frontmatter and top-level sections.
Frontmatter must contain: generated_at_commit: <git rev-parse HEAD> generated_on: <YYYY-MM-DD> survey_scope: src/, test/**/runtests.jl, examples/**/*.jl, docs/src/**/*.md, **/Project.toml
Top-level sections (in order): ## Modules ## Exported symbols ## Environments (Project.toml table) ## Figures in docs/src/assets/ ## Figure-placement issues (omit section if empty)
Mark any entry that cannot be confirmed with evidence as: INCOMPLETE: <reason> Never invent entries.

Spot-check accuracy: pick 20 random rows from JUMP.md.
For each row, confirm the claim by reading the exact line: sed -n '<line>p' <file>
The symbol or @testset name must appear verbatim in the output. If a mismatch is found: 1. Fix the JUMP.md row (correct the line number or remove the entry and mark INCOMPLETE). 2. Fix the corresponding per-file summary if it also cited the wrong line.
If JUMP.md has fewer than 20 rows, spot-check every row. Do NOT declare the run complete until all 20 (or all, if fewer) checks pass.

rubric.criterion: Every symbol → file:line claim in JUMP.md has been verified by the 20-row spot-check. No entry points to a line that does not contain the claimed symbol. No entries are fabricated — items that cannot be confirmed are marked INCOMPLETE.

Every src/*.jl, every test/**/runtests.jl, every examples/**/*.jl (excluding output/), and every docs/src/**/*.md has a per-file summary. Every concrete type, every exported symbol, and every @testset has a JUMP.md row. Every Project.toml is listed in INDEX.md. Every figure is either listed under "Figures in docs/src/assets/" or flagged under "Figure-placement issues".

Artifacts are scanable without scrolling. INDEX.md fits in one screen for a typical repo. JUMP.md uses compact tables (one row per entry, no blank rows between entries). Per-file summaries use bullet lists or tables, not paragraphs.
In incremental mode, files outside the diff are byte-identical before and after the run. git diff --stat on the artifact directory shows only the files actually updated.
Entries the agent cannot confirm with evidence (e.g., a file that is empty or a symbol whose line cannot be determined) are marked "INCOMPLETE: <reason>" and never invented.
Each source file is read at most once per run. In incremental mode, only files in the diff are read. No bulk re-read of unchanged files.

Multiple-dispatch method clusters are grouped by function name. Base method extensions are recorded explicitly with the Base function name as the grouping key. Bare type names (no {T,...} parameters) are used in headings, matching the base-show.xml convention.

The only files written are under ai/repo-summary-<date>/. On first install, ai/README.md and .claude/settings.json are also edited (one-time). No package source, test, example, or docs file is modified.

formatting.rule: All artifacts are GitHub-flavored Markdown, no emojis.

INDEX.md must begin with YAML frontmatter (triple-dash delimiters): --- generated_at_commit: <40-char SHA> generated_on: <YYYY-MM-DD> survey_scope: src/, test/**/runtests.jl, examples/**/*.jl, docs/src/**/*.md, **/Project.toml ---

JUMP.md uses four-column tables for symbols and types: | symbol | kind | file | line | and three-column tables for testsets: | testset | file | line | and two-column tables for task lookup: | task / concept | relevant files | One row per entry. No blank rows between entries within a section.

SEMANTIC.md uses ## headings per group. Each entry under a heading is a bullet: - `BareSymbol` (relative/path/file.jl:line) — one-line role description The bare symbol name uses no type parameters. Roles are ten words or fewer.

Per-file summary template (ai/repo-summary-<date>/files/<flattened-path>.md): **Purpose:** <one line>
**Key types:** (src/ files only — bullet list with file:line) **Key functions / testsets:** (bullet list with file:line) **Dependencies:** (Julia packages from `using`; intra-repo files from `include`; nearest Project.toml for examples)
Mark missing information as INCOMPLETE: <reason>.
Bare type names in all headings and table cells — never Foo{T,S,P}. Type parameters may be mentioned in body prose only if essential for disambiguation.
Figure-placement warnings use the exact string: INCOMPLETE: figure outside docs/src/assets/: <path-from-repo-root> so that future grep-based cleanup passes can find them reliably.

Flattened path convention for per-file summaries: replace every `/` in the relative path with `__`, drop the file extension, and append `.md`. Examples: src/ParameterDistributions.jl → files/src__ParameterDistributions.md test/EnsembleKalmanProcess/runtests.jl → files/test__EnsembleKalmanProcess__runtests.md examples/Lorenz/calibrate.jl → files/examples__Lorenz__calibrate.md docs/src/API/Inversion.md → files/docs__src__API__Inversion.md
formatting.example:
## INDEX.md (excerpt)
--- generated_at_commit: abc123def456abc123def456abc123def456abc1 generated_on: 2026-05-09 survey_scope: src/, test/**/runtests.jl, examples/**/*.jl, docs/src/**/*.md, **/Project.toml ---
## Modules | module | file | line | |--------|------|------| | MyModule | src/MyModule.jl | 1 | | MySubModule | src/MySubModule.jl | 1 |
## Environments | path | role | |------|------| | Project.toml | root package | | docs/Project.toml | documentation build | | test/Project.toml | test environment | | examples/MyExample/Project.toml | example: MyExample |
---
## JUMP.md (excerpt)
## Types | symbol | kind | file | line | |--------|------|------|------| | MyProcess | struct | src/MyProcess.jl | 42 | | AbstractProcess | abstract type | src/MyProcess.jl | 10 |
## Testsets | testset | file | line | |---------|------|------| | "MyProcess construction" | test/MyProcess/runtests.jl | 15 | | "MyProcess update step" | test/MyProcess/runtests.jl | 28 |
## Multi-dispatch clusters | function | file | line | |----------|------|------| | update! | src/ProcessA.jl | 77 | | update! | src/ProcessB.jl | 33 |
---
## Per-file summary: files/src__MyProcess.md
**Purpose:** Defines MyProcess, the core update loop, and its Base.show method.
**Key types:** - `MyProcess` (src/MyProcess.jl:42) — concrete process struct - `AbstractProcess` (src/MyProcess.jl:10) — supertype for all processes
**Key functions / testsets:** - `update!` (src/MyProcess.jl:77) — one EKI update step - `Base.show` (src/MyProcess.jl:120) — REPL display - `Base.summary` (src/MyProcess.jl:115) — one-line header
**Dependencies:** LinearAlgebra, Statistics; includes src/utils.jl
